pattern: Maximizing a product of edge probabilities along a path is a shortest-path problem in disguise: relax neighbors with the product instead of the sum, and always expand the currently most-probable frontier node first.
hints: Treat each edge's success probability as a weight and build an undirected weighted graph.
This is Dijkstra's algorithm with 'distance' replaced by 'probability': instead of adding weights and minimizing, multiply them and maximize.

Keep a best-probability-so-far array (1.0 at the start node, 0 elsewhere) and a max-priority frontier; whenever going through the current node gives a neighbor a higher product than its recorded best, update it and re-queue that neighbor.

edge_inputs.description: a direct edge beats a longer indirect path
minimum graph size with one edge
no edges at all between the two nodes
a zero-probability edge must never be chosen when a direct alternative exists
querying a node against itself is trivially probability one
two disconnected components can never reach each other

stress.description: a random spanning tree plus extra random edges up to the edge budget, near the maximum node count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 10000))
    nodes = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(nodes)
    edges = []
    succ = []
    seen = set()
    for i in range(1, n):
        j = rng.randrange(i)
        a, b = nodes[i], nodes[j]
        edges.append([a, b])
        succ.append(round(rng.uniform(0.1, 1.0), 3))
        seen.add(tuple(sorted((a, b))))
    budget = min(20000, n * 2) - (n - 1)
    attempts = 0
    while budget > 0 and attempts < budget * 4:
        a = rng.randrange(n)
        b = rng.randrange(n)
        attempts += 1
        if a == b:
            continue
        key = tuple(sorted((a, b)))
        if key in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(key)
        edges.append([a, b])
        succ.append(round(rng.uniform(0.1, 1.0), 3))
        budget -= 1
    start_node = rng.randrange(n)
    end_node = rng.randrange(n)
    while n > 1 and end_node == start_node:
        end_node = rng.randrange(n)
    return [n, edges, succ, start_node, end_node]
